settings.title: Unlock The Hidden Messages
settings.description:
Stories old, told anew.  

Throughout the ages, lovers have exchanged intimate, secret messages through the language of jewellery. As tokens of shared sentiment, our latest capsule of 10 one-of-a-kind heirloom designs, each have a story to tell.  

Designed as an opportunity to mark the next chapter in your story, or begin one anew. More Than Yesterday, is a celebration of the union of past and present and an invitation to forge your own legacy.  

We believe every piece of jewellery is a talisman for personal expression, honouring the ways in which our creations become conduits for connection. More Than Yesterday is full of meaning—with secret compartments, hidden engravings, and rich symbolism—designed to convey a story as unique as the wearer.

Here, we decode the significance of each design.

settings.title: 'More Than Yesterday' Ring
settings.description:
“For, you see, each day I love you more, today more than yesterday and less than tomorrow.” 

Inspired by the 1889 poem “The Eternal Song” by Rosemonde Gérard, the hero piece from this collection uses a filigree-inspired gallery to reveal a secret message to the wearer.  

Filigree, a delicate and intricate metalwork technique, has been used in jewellery for centuries. Made by twisting fine threads of gold or silver into lace-like patterns, filigree creates ornate, airy designs that often carry hidden meanings.

An enchanting post-consumer Old Mine Cut Diamond represents ‘Yesterday’ having been given new life through this one-of-a-kind design.

settings.title: 'As Long As There Is Love' Solitaire
settings.description:
Ruby, with its rich red hue, has long been a totem of love, passion, and power. Known as the ‘king of gemstones,’ it has been treasured across cultures for its deep, fiery colour, often associated with the heart.

In ancient times, rubies were believed to protect warriors in battle and ward off evil. But they also illustrated vitality and life force, making them popular in romantic jewellery as tokens of intense love and commitment.

Wrapping wire work, and small metal details paired with our Vieira Setting, a Natalie Marie Jewellery take on a vintage scalloped setting, this piece encourages us to think of all the elements involved in love, and act as a reminder of their importance as a whole.

settings.title: 'Ruler Of My Heart'
settings.description:
An opportunity to cherish your loved one, always keeping them close by, the Ruler Of My Heart Locket features a heart-shaped window to store whatever you desire.

Originating in the 16th century, lockets were initially worn to hold small mementos, like locks of hair, messages, or perfumed sachets, before becoming popular in the Victorian era for containing a portrait of a loved one. One of the earliest known examples of this was Queen Elizabeth I’s locket ring, originally commissioned for her in 1575.

Our Ruler Of My Heart Locket uses symbolism of a heart and crown, dating back to the Victorian era, symbolising the wearer as “the ruler of my heart”. A symbol of love and a promise of an everlasting bond shouted from the rooftops.

settings.title: 'Forte' Signet
settings.description:
A carnelian is a vibrant umber stone, reminiscent of our Australian natural landscape, used in jewellery throughout the Middle Ages and believed to embody courage, bravery and strength. 

Our Forte Signet Ring, inspired by the Italian word meaning ‘strong’, is designed to be worn as a reminder of the meaning it holds, with metal work surrounding the carnelian embodying this storyline.

Our Forte Signet Ring, inspired by the Italian word meaning ‘strong’, is designed to be worn as a reminder of the meaning it holds, with metal work surrounding the carnelian embodying this storyline.

settings.title: 'Key To My Heart' Ring
settings.description:
In the Victorian era, jewellers saw an opportunity to hide symbolic motifs within their creations by using intricate wire work to create distinct patterns. These lace-like designs would feature in romantic rings, where hearts, flowers and other shapes often appeared.

The Key To My Heart ring features an old mine-cut pear diamond set within intricate wire work, representing the way a turning key unlocks a door, revealing what waits beyond.

settings.title: 'As Deep As The Ocean' Halo Ring
settings.description:
Gemstones have always been carefully chosen by jewellery makers for the secret meaning they carry.  Historically speaking, deep blue sapphires were believed to express emotional depth and trust, evoking a sense of divine connection and protection. A rich blue Ceylon sapphire takes centre stage in our As Deep As The Ocean ring, representing a love—whether for the self or other—that runs as deep as the ocean.  

 Inspired by free thinkers, As Deep As The Ocean is a piece daring to be brave, bold and different.

 Inspired by free thinkers, As Deep As The Ocean is a piece daring to be brave, bold and different.

settings.title: 'Ivy' Wrap Ring
settings.description:
A token of a deep rooted love, Ivy symbolises an inseparable bond, a promise of endless devotion.  

Green gemstones have been valued in jewellery for centuries, embodying nature, growth, and renewal. Emeralds, revered by ancient Egyptians and Incas, were associated with fertility, rebirth, and eternal youth, so have come to typify a love that transcends the seasons of our lifetimes. Accentuated by seven green emeralds to portray a love undying, the Ivy ring embodies a promise of forever.

Accentuated by seven green emeralds to portray a love undying, the Ivy ring embodies a promise of forever.

settings.title: 'Crescent Moon' Shield Ring
settings.description:
The crescent moon is a symbol of femininity, representing the transition from one phase of life to another.

Inspired by Warren B Davis’ artwork ‘Crescent Moon’ circa 1895, which features a poetic feminine shape, and it’s intrinsic connection to the cycles of the moon.

In ancient cultures, the crescent moon was associated with goddesses, fertility, and the cyclical nature of life, reflecting the waxing and waning phases of the moon. In vintage jewellery, this motif has a rich history of celebrating womanhood and is used within our Crescent Moon ring as an indication of the power of feminine energy.

settings.title: 'Forget Me Not'
settings.description: During wartime, jewellery contained concealed messages or codes, sent to lovers as a romantic token. Our Forget Me Not ring features a hidden hinge detail, revealing a swallow in flight holding a Ceylon sapphire to represent a Forget-Me-Not flower. The intention is to convey the phrase, 'I am thinking of you until I return', a way to mark time until lovers are reunited.

settings.title: 'Be Mine' Ring
settings.description:
Rooted in Irish folklore, the rare four-leaf clover is said to bring good fortune, with each leaf standing for faith, hope, love, and luck. During the Victorian era, four-leaf clover motifs became popular in jewellery design, often given as tokens of affection and good wishes.

They also conveyed a simple message 'be mine'. With graduating layers either side of an emerald cut green sapphire representing the sacred four leaves of a clover, our Be Mine Ring is a gesture of devotion.
